behaviors.description: Cache keys generated from identical query parameters are always identical, and keys from different parameters never collide.
behaviors.justification: The core failure boundary is deterministic key-generation logic (normalization/serialization/hashing) inside one local component. No real I/O is required to expose real bugs like order-sensitivity or unstable serialization.
behaviors.test_description: Unit/property tests for `buildCacheKey(params)`: assert same logical params (including permuted field order) always produce the same key, and a large generated corpus of distinct params does not produce collisions. Include edge cases (nulls, arrays, nested objects, numeric/string ambiguities).

behaviors.description: The TTL for a cache entry is computed correctly based on the data category's configured freshness rule (e.g., user profiles = 5 minutes, product catalog = 1 hour).
behaviors.justification: This behavior targets rule lookup and TTL calculation logic. Real failures here are typically mapping/default/math errors within local code, not infrastructure boundaries.
behaviors.test_description: Unit tests for `computeTTL(category, freshnessConfig, now)`: verify expected TTL per category, boundary/default behavior for unknown category, and time arithmetic correctness. Freeze time to avoid flakiness.

behaviors.description: When memory usage exceeds the configured threshold, the eviction strategy switches from LRU to LFU as specified in the configuration.
behaviors.justification: The failure mode is at module boundaries: memory-pressure signal + config + policy selector + cache runtime state. A pure unit test of selector logic can miss wiring/propagation defects.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test with real cache modules wired together and a controllable memory-metrics provider. Start below threshold (assert LRU active), raise reported usage above threshold (assert LFU becomes active), then lower usage if hysteresis behavior exists. Verify strategy observed through actual eviction outcomes, not only an internal flag.

behaviors.description: A write to the underlying PostgreSQL table invalidates all cache entries whose keys match the affected rows.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on real PostgreSQL write semantics and invalidation propagation path (triggers/listen-notify/CDC/transaction timing). Mocking DB events can hide real failures.
behaviors.test_description: System test using a real PostgreSQL instance: seed rows and corresponding cache entries, execute real SQL write/update/delete in a transaction, then assert matching cache keys are invalidated while unrelated keys remain. Include commit/rollback timing checks if invalidation is commit-driven.

behaviors.description: The eviction policy removes the correct entries — least-recently-used under LRU, least-frequently-used under LFU — when the cache is full.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on interaction between access tracking, frequency/recency counters, and eviction execution in the cache component. Isolated policy-unit tests can miss state-update wiring bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test for in-memory cache stack: fill cache to capacity, apply controlled access patterns, insert one extra item, and assert exact key evicted under LRU; repeat under LFU with known frequency distribution (including tie-breaker expectation). Validate through public cache API/state, not mocked internals.
